channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Russia's cyberwar on Europe: UK, EU strike back with joint sanctions, summon Kremlin's envoys
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: This AI founder walked away from a $300,000 life in San Francisco: 'No money could make me stay here'
item.description: Koby Conrad, founder of AI sobriety companion Sunflower, relocated to Buenos Aires. He found a better environment for building a family and his company. Conrad enjoys the city's culture, food, and significantly lower living costs. He feels more focused and productive away from San Francisco's tech-centric atmosphere. The move supports his desire to balance ambitious work with raising a family.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 18 ancient Egyptian tombs found with golden tongues
item.description: Recently, a remarkable discovery was made with the unearthing of eighteen ancient Egyptian tombs near Marina el-Alamein. Among the intriguing artifacts found were sealed stone slabs, an impressive granite coffin, and dozens of gold tongues strategically placed in the mouths of mummies to assist them in speaking in the afterlife. Additionally, an intricately designed offering altar and a statue of Aphrodite were also uncovered, shedding light on ancient burial practices.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Why Prince George wore suit at Wimbledon, even in the heat
item.description: Prince George and Prince William attended the Wimbledon men's final in formal attire. They adhered to the Royal Box dress code, which mandates suits and ties. Despite the heat, this strict protocol was followed by the royal attendees. Children are rarely permitted in the exclusive Royal Box seating area. However, Prince George and Princess Charlotte were exceptions to this unwritten rule.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: In 1906, one wrong turn sent the SS Valencia into the Pacific's “Graveyard of the Pacific”, where more than 100 people died
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 65-year-old grandfather attacked by a Yellowstone bison was cracking jokes despite breaking multiple bones
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: A hidden cave in Norway protected a 75,000-year-old Arctic world until scientists finally uncovered it
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Richard Feynman's unsent love letter to his late wife shows that love doesn't always end with goodbye
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: In 1883, Hawaii introduced mongooses to control rats in sugarcane fields. They soon became a far bigger threat to native wildlife
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet Victoria Gowramma: Indian princess who grew up inside Buckingham Palace and became the first royal to convert to Christianity, had Queen Victoria as her godmother
item.description: The tale of Princess Gowramma, daughter of the last Raja of Kodagu, begins with her baptism in London, where Queen Victoria assumed the role of her godmother and named her Victoria Gowramma. After marrying Colonel John Campbell and having a daughter, she encountered societal bias due to her appearance. Tragically, Princess Victoria Gowramma passed away from tuberculosis, her remarkable story fading into obscurity over time.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Science word of the day: Allele, know its meaning, who introduced the term, and its practical applications
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: 8-year-old Florida boy collected over 50 robot vacuums, learned to repair them and built a YouTube brand that earned up to $1,800 in a month: Meet Wyatt Biggs
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'We heard an explosion, then flames spread': Survivors recount Bangkok pub inferno that killed 27
item.description: In a horrific incident, a raging fire swept through a crowded pub in Bangkok, resulting in the deaths of 27 individuals. Panic ensued as smoke enveloped the venue, with numerous victims cornered at the rear due to obstructed emergency exits. Eyewitnesses shared their desperate attempts to escape the chaos as the inferno blazed shortly before midnight.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Winston Churchill: "Personally, I'm always ready to learn, although I do not…" - know why real learning often begins when comfort ends
item.description: Winston Churchill's famous quote highlights the human preference for learning over being taught. He acknowledged the discomfort of admitting gaps in one's own understanding. True growth requires embracing correction, even when it feels unpleasant. Curiosity and humility are essential partners for continuous improvement. This timeless wisdom remains relevant in today's information-rich world.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Michael Jordan: "I can accept failure, everyone fails at something. But…" - the life lesson that proves regret often lasts longer than failure
item.description: Michael Jordan's quote highlights that trying is more important than fearing failure. He accepted failure as a normal part of any worthwhile endeavor. Jordan's own career demonstrates numerous setbacks and relentless effort. The greatest obstacle is often the fear of beginning something new. Every setback offers an opportunity for improvement and learning.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Europe death toll tops 10,000 during late-June heatwave; scientists blame human-caused climate change
item.description: Europe recorded over ten thousand excess deaths during a late June heatwave. Older populations, aged sixty-five and above, were most severely impacted by the extreme heat. Scientists link this heatwave's intensity and frequency to human-caused climate change. France and Belgium reported very high excess mortality during the final week of June.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Smoke was everywhere': How Bangkok's deadly pub fire unfolded; 27 killed – 10 key points
item.description: Tragedy struck at Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao pub, where a fierce fire resulted in the deaths of twenty-seven people. Victims found themselves ensnared in the pub's bathrooms as they tried to escape. Smoke filled the venue quickly, contributing to the catastrophe. Emergency responders faced significant hurdles when attempting to rescue those inside. Authorities have launched an investigation to uncover what led to this heartbreaking event.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Zelenskyy ousts Ukrainian PM as part of broader cabinet reshuffle
item.description: Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko has stepped down from her position. President Zelenskyy announced fresh government changes and a new role for the former premier. Svyrydenko expressed pride in leading the government during difficult times. She stated that she discussed next steps with President Zelenskyy. The President indicated a shift in the nation's political strategy.

item.title: Iran newspaper publishes list of 13 foreign leaders as revenge targets
item.description: A conservative Iranian newspaper published a revenge list targeting foreign leaders. This list includes prominent figures from the United States and Israel. Mojtaba Khamenei stated vengeance is the nation's will and must be carried out. The infographic featured leaders like Trump, Netanyahu, and Macron. These individuals will face consequences for the supreme leader's killing.

item.title: 'We want you tomorrow': Bangladesh official slams Sheikh Hasina's delayed return
item.description: Bangladesh's International Crimes Tribunal prosecutor wants Sheikh Hasina to surrender immediately. She faces a death sentence and jail upon returning from India. The prosecutor stated Hasina would be sent directly to jail without court appearance. He warned against any stunts or creating further instability in the country. The tribunal's activities have gained momentum under the current government.
item.dc:creator: Ahsan Tasnim
